skill.name: ai-seo
skill.description: Optimize content so AI search engines and assistants can understand, extract, and cite it in generated answers.
skill.summary: Improve AI search visibility with audits, citation-focused content patterns, and platform-specific guidance.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found documentation, markdown templates, SEO guidance, eval text, and relative reference links rather than executable code. No evidence found of command execution, weak cryptography use, network exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The only real operational concern is a low-risk instruction to read an optional local marketing context file.

low_findings.title: Optional Workspace Context Read
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to read .agents/product-marketing-context.md or .claude/product-marketing-context.md if present. This is local workspace context access for personalization, not exfiltration or command execution, but it may expose sensitive marketing context to the active model session.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read instruction is explicit and limited to known local context files. I found no network transmission, shell execution, or attempt to access secrets.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Guidance
low_findings.description: The static high and medium findings resolve to markdown examples, SEO terms, eval expectations, platform guidance, and documentation links. The reviewed locations do not contain executable crypto calls, shell commands, script blocks, network scanners, or malicious instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files are markdown or eval data, and the cited lines were manually reviewed. Their context confirms documentation patterns rather than live code paths.

content.value_statement: AI search engines often cite content that is easy to extract, verify, and trust. This skill helps teams audit AI visibility and reshape content for citation in AI-generated answers.
actual_capabilities: Guides AI visibility audits across Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot.
Provides content patterns for definitions, steps, comparisons, FAQs, statistics, quotes, and evidence-backed claims.
Explains robots.txt tradeoffs for AI crawler access and citation visibility.
Recommends content formats that AI systems are more likely to cite, including comparisons and definitive guides.
Maps platform-specific ranking factors to practical optimization actions.
Suggests monitoring workflows and tools for tracking AI citations over time.
limitations: It does not perform live searches unless the host AI tool has browsing or search access.
It does not implement schema markup directly; it refers users to a structured data workflow.
AI search platforms change often, so important claims should be verified before publication.
It focuses on AI citation readiness, not a full technical SEO audit.

use_cases.title: Audit AI Visibility for a SaaS Brand
use_cases.description: Check whether important product queries cite the brand across AI search platforms and identify missing structure, authority, and presence signals.

use_cases.title: Rewrite Pages for AI Citation
use_cases.description: Convert existing pages into extractable answer blocks, comparison tables, FAQs, and sourced claims that AI systems can cite.

use_cases.title: Plan AI Search Monitoring
use_cases.description: Create a recurring process for tracking AI Overview presence, brand citation rate, share of AI voice, and cited source pages.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner AI Visibility Check
prompt_templates.scenario: A team wants a first look at AI search readiness.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our content for AI search readiness. Focus on whether our pages have clear answers, cited sources, schema opportunities, and AI crawler access.

prompt_templates.title: Content Rewrite for Citation
prompt_templates.scenario: A writer wants to improve one page for AI extraction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page and suggest changes that make it more likely to be cited by AI assistants. Include answer blocks, FAQ ideas, statistics, and source improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive AI Search Gap
prompt_templates.scenario: A marketer sees competitors cited in AI answers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare our content against competitors that appear in AI answers for these queries. Identify gaps in structure, authority, third-party presence, and freshness.

prompt_templates.title: Platform-Specific AI SEO Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user wants an execution roadmap by platform.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a platform-specific AI SEO plan for Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot. Prioritize actions by impact and implementation effort.

output_examples.input: Audit our project management software pages for AI search visibility.
output: A platform-by-platform visibility checklist for major AI search engines.
A prioritized list of missing extractable answers, source citations, schema opportunities, and crawler access checks.
A short action plan for improving citation readiness over the next month.

output_examples.input: Make this comparison article more likely to appear in AI answers.
output: A revised page structure with a direct answer, fair comparison criteria, and clearer headings.
Recommendations for statistics, expert quotes, and source links that strengthen citation quality.
FAQ questions that match natural AI search queries.

output_examples.input: Should we block AI crawlers in robots.txt?
output: A clear explanation of the tradeoff between content protection and AI citation visibility.
A list of relevant AI user agents to review with engineering and legal teams.
A decision framework based on brand goals, risk tolerance, and search visibility needs.

best_practices: Start with real priority queries, then test whether AI platforms cite your brand or competitors.
Put direct answers, dates, sources, and author credentials near the content they support.
Use structured formats such as tables, numbered steps, FAQs, and self-contained answer blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not stuff AI SEO keywords into thin content without useful evidence or clear answers.
Do not block AI crawlers without understanding the effect on citation visibility.
Do not rely only on your own site when AI systems also cite third-party mentions and review platforms.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps teams optimize content so AI assistants and AI search engines can understand, extract, and cite it.

faq.question: Does it replace traditional SEO?
faq.answer: No. It builds on SEO foundations and focuses on citation readiness, answer extraction, and AI platform visibility.

faq.question: Can it check live AI search results?
faq.answer: Only if the host tool has search or browsing access. Otherwise, it provides a manual audit process.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, and related AI crawler considerations.

faq.question: Is it useful for technical SEO?
faq.answer: It can identify related issues, but full technical SEO audits should use a dedicated SEO audit workflow.

faq.question: Does it write schema markup?
faq.answer: It recommends schema opportunities, but implementation should use a structured data or schema markup workflow.
